title: Lesson Quiz: Researching Career Information
description: Assessment of the concept that Job roles, responsibilities, and requirements.

questions.prompt: What does career research help a student understand?

choices.feedback: Correct. Career research helps students learn what a job involves and what is needed to prepare for it.
Career research is broader than deciding what seems easiest.
Career research should rely on information, not only opinions from friends.

choices.text: Only what hobbies a student has
choices.feedback: Hobbies may connect to careers, but career research focuses on job information.

questions.prompt: A job responsibility is one of the duties a worker is expected to complete.

choices.feedback: Responsibilities are the duties and tasks connected to a job.
Correct. Job responsibilities describe what a worker is expected to do.

questions.prompt: Which example best shows a career requirement?

choices.text: The color of a company's logo
choices.feedback: A company logo is not a career requirement.
A lunch schedule is not a requirement for entering a career.

choices.text: The education, training, or skill needed for a job
choices.feedback: Correct. A career requirement is the preparation or skill needed for a role.
A favorite subject may connect to a career, but it is not itself a requirement.

questions.prompt: Why is it useful to research a career before deciding whether to explore it further?

choices.text: It removes the need to compare other options
choices.feedback: Students should still compare multiple options.

choices.text: It guarantees a final choice immediately
choices.feedback: Career research helps with decision-making, but it does not guarantee an immediate final choice.

choices.text: It helps students gather useful facts before making decisions
choices.feedback: Accurate information helps, but this choice does not best explain why research supports informed decisions.

choices.text: It helps students make more informed career decisions using real information
choices.feedback: Correct. Career research gives students accurate information they can use when making decisions.

questions.prompt: Researching career information can help a student compare different job options more effectively.

choices.feedback: Correct. Career research helps students compare options using facts instead of guesses.
Career research is useful because it supports comparison and better decision-making.
